params.text:
What RIDDOR requires

RIDDOR 2013 requires the responsible person — usually the employer or the person in control of the premises — to report certain serious work-related events to the HSE. Front-line staff are not expected to report to the HSE themselves, but you must pass information up so the responsible person can decide.

The main reportable categories are:

  • Deaths arising from a work-related accident.
  • Specified injuries — such as fractures (other than to fingers, thumbs or toes), amputations, permanent loss of sight, crush injuries to the head or torso, serious burns, scalpings, and loss of consciousness from a head injury or asphyxia.
  • Over-seven-day injuries — where a worker cannot do their normal duties for more than seven consecutive days.
  • Reportable occupational diseases — such as occupational dermatitis, severe cramp of the hand or forearm, and certain infections linked to work.
  • Dangerous occurrences — specified near-miss events with high potential to cause harm, for example the collapse or failure of lifting equipment.

params.alt: Illustration for RIDDOR: what to report

params.question: Under RIDDOR, who is normally responsible for making the report to the HSE?

answers.text: The responsible person — usually the employer or the person in control of the premises.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. The duty to report to the HSE sits with the responsible person, not with the individual care worker.

answers.text: Every member of care staff must report directly to the HSE themselves.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. Individual staff pass information to the responsible person, who decides on RIDDOR reporting.

answers.text: The person who was injured must report their own injury to the HSE.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. The legal duty rests with the responsible person, typically the employer.

answers.text: No one — RIDDOR reporting is optional in care settings.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: No. RIDDOR is a legal requirement and reporting the specified events is mandatory.
